interactions.summary: Jiang completes the transnational-capital answer by predicting civil war across the West and even US absorption of Canada.
The host asks whether a new order emerges from those ashes.
Jiang answers with a new order centered on Israel, Middle East demolition and reconstruction, and redirected transnational capital.
The host asks which global theater is most consequential among the many active conflict zones.
Jiang says Iran is the decisive theater and a China-Japan conflict is second because Taiwan and sea-lane control determine Japan’s survival.
The host presses Jiang on why Taiwan is necessary for a Chinese embargo of Japan.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts transnational capital’s endgame is to let Europe and America descend into civil war while preserving capital elsewhere.

Jiang’s proposed postwar order is Pax Judaica, with Israel becoming the new center of gravity after Iran and the surrounding Middle East are devastated and rebuilt with imported labor and redirected capital.
Jiang predicts the United States will enter Iran, devastate the Iranian state, then be forced into withdrawal by an unwinnable war and domestic civil conflict.
Jiang says a later China-Japan confrontation would be driven by Japan’s need to preserve freedom of navigation and avoid dependence on a China-controlled Taiwan chokepoint.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang imagines a peace built not on reconciliation but on demolition, cheap labor, and capital flight into a remade Greater Israel.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Gaza has been bulldozed. It's not a war. It's a demolition
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the brutal physical image behind his Pax Judaica thesis.

signature_moments.moment: Iran is elevated from one conflict zone to the place that decides the world order for half a century.
signature_moments.source_phrase: What happens in Iran will determine the world order for the next 50 years
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is one of the interview’s strongest chronology-and-scale claims.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang’s term for a postwar order in which Israel becomes the new strategic and financial center of gravity.
Used here as the strategic condition Japan must preserve to avoid starvation through blockade.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang orders the future as Iran first and China-Japan second, with the Iran war establishing the longer postwar architecture he calls Pax Judaica.

uncertainty_notes.note: The packet contains maximal speculative forecasts about civil wars, CENTCOM, and a Greater Israel order; these are Jiang’s forward model, not source-verified outcomes.
